name: Q356886
abstract: Rossiere "Shadow" Wilson (September 25, 1919 – July 11, 1959) was an American jazz drummer. Much of Wilson's early work was with swing jazz orchestras.

concerts.label: Fred Robbins One-Nite Stand: Illinois Jacquet and His Orchestra with Ella Fitzgerald
Jazz Concert with Sarah Vaughan, Erroll Garner, Lester Young and Others
Midnight Performance: Thanksgiving Jazz at Carnegie Hall

about_quotes.speaker: Jones Hank
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with Shadow Wilson at a place up… It was at Mittens. ... I worked there with a group with Jerome Richardson, Wendell Marshall, and Shadow Wilson.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jones Hank — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Red Callender
about_quotes.evidence: That was with Herbie Haymer, Arnold Ross, Les Paul, Shadow Wilson. That's when we did "These Foolish Things"

about_quotes.speaker: Gene Ramey
about_quotes.evidence: Shadow Wilson came in when I was working with Basie... had Shadow Wilson come in and play the show... the best bass solo I ever took with Shadow Wilson.

about_quotes.speaker: Big Nick Nicholas
about_quotes.evidence: Billy Eckstine was the star and they had ... Shadow Wilson was the drummer and I forget the bass player's name... It was a good band.

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: And Shadow Wilson on drums. ... We had in this band a little tenor [saxophone] player by the name of Willie Parker.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John Malachi
about_quotes.evidence: I experienced playing with Shadow Wilson, which I felt like was one of the greatest drummers I'd ever played with.
